meta.title: Range Rover Evoque L538 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.h1: Range Rover Evoque L538 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the first-generation Evoque L538 — from the 2.2-litre TD4 diesel to the 2.0-litre Si4 petrol and the later Ingenium diesel that arrived with the 2015 facelift. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and a style icon's engine story. Backed by 244+ real UK L538 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Range Rover Evoque L538 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Evoque L538 specifically — including the Ford-PSA DW12 diesel, the EcoBoost-derived Si4 petrol, and the later Ingenium diesel that arrived with the 2015 facelift.

overview.intro: The first-generation Range Rover Evoque (codename L538) debuted in July 2011 as Land Rover's entry into the compact luxury crossover segment. Built at Halewood alongside the Freelander 2, it was based on a modified Ford EUCD platform (codenamed D8). The engine range initially comprised a 2.2-litre turbodiesel from the Ford-PSA DW12 family and a 2.0-litre turbocharged petrol from Ford's EcoBoost family. A significant 2015 facelift brought Land Rover's new Ingenium engine family to the range — 2.0-litre diesels and petrols replacing the older Ford-sourced units. The L538 was offered in three-door coupé, five-door, and from 2016, two-door convertible body styles.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Ford EUCD (D8 platform) • Predecessor: Freelander (spiritual) • Successor: Evoque L551 (2018–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: 3-door Coupé, 5-door SUV, 2-door Convertible.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The Evoque L538 generated 244 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 2.2 TD4 diesel and 2.0 Si4 petrol as these stylish crossovers enter the higher-mileage phase of their ownership cycle.

items.quote: Ford-PSA's DW12 is a proven workhorse — the same engine that powered countless Ford, Peugeot, and Citroën vehicles, as well as the Freelander 2. EGR and DPF are the only real demands; the bottom end is genuinely durable.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want proven diesel reliability without Ingenium complexity.

items.quote: Ford's EcoBoost 2.0 is a well-understood turbo petrol with strong parts availability. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity — the engine itself is robust.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want style and pace without diesel costs.

items.quote: Early Ingenium diesels (pre-2019) carry timing chain tensioner risk and oil dilution from DPF regeneration. These are the most complex engines in the L538 range and require specialist attention.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the latest diesel tech and are prepared for specialist maintenance.

items.quote: Timing chain and EGR cooler failures can run into thousands; Ingenium diesels are significantly more expensive to repair than the older 2.2 DW12. Full replacement costs £3,500–£6,000.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with full service history and a specialist budget.

items.quote: The most common L538 diesel by far, with cheap parts, nationwide specialist coverage, and a well-understood failure profile. A £2,000–£3,500 recon makes financial sense against a £5,000–£14,000 vehicle.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most cost-effective Evoque ownership.

items.quote: The Ingenium petrol is more reliable than its diesel stablemate — no DPF, no EGR, and the cooling system issues are well-documented and manageable. A facelift Si4 is a genuinely solid choice.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want a modern petrol Evoque without the diesel's caveats.

ownershipEconomics.h2: Range Rover Evoque L538 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — proven DW12 diesel; cheap to run
Solid Choice — Ingenium petrol more reliable than diesel

economicsRule.title: The L538 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,000–£3,500 2.2 TD4 recon against a £5,000–£14,000 Evoque sits at 14–70% of the car's value. Against a sub-£6,000 high-mileage example, that brushes our 60% Land Rover threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £8,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 25–44% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

problems.title: 224DT DW12 EGR Cooler & DPF Clogging
problems.rootCause: The DW12's EGR cooler can develop internal leaks, allowing coolant into the exhaust; DPF clogging is common in urban-use vehicles. Injector seal leaks (carbon buildup) are also a known pattern.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of EGR cooler leaks (coolant loss, white smoke) or DPF warning lights, get it inspected immediately — a £400–£900 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 replacement that follows if ignored. On an L538 worth under £6,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare 224DT engine prices for your Evoque →

problems.title: 204DTD/204DTA Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Tensioner
problems.rootCause: Early Ingenium diesels (pre-2019) suffer from timing chain tensioner wear — a design flaw improved in later production. Oil dilution from DPF regeneration accelerates wear. Cold-start rattle is the classic early warning.
problems.recommendation: If your L538 Ingenium diesel has no documented timing chain history, treat it as a priority inspection — £800–£1,600 now is far cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows a failure. Cold-start rattle is the warning sign to watch for.

cta.label: Compare 204DTD engine prices for your Evoque →

problems.title: 204PT/PT204 EcoBoost/Ingenium Petrol Cooling System
problems.rootCause: The 2.0-litre turbo petrol engines (both EcoBoost and Ingenium) have known cooling system issues — water pump failure, coolant leaks from plastic fittings, and thermostat housing cracks are the most common. Overheating can lead to head gasket failure if ignored.
problems.recommendation: If your L538 Si4 has no documented cooling system history, treat water pump replacement as preventative maintenance at 60,000 miles — £400–£1,000 now is far cheaper than the £3,000+ replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare 204PT engine prices for your Evoque →

replacementCosts.h2: L538 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Engine Page baseline — no dedicated Engine Pages exist for these families yet, so baseline figures are drawn from the EM_Proprietary_Data Table A. Labour hours estimated at 10–14 hours for all L538 engines due to transverse mounting and Halewood production access.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are first-generation Evoque L538 only. The eD4 is a two-wheel-drive variant introduced in 2012; TD4 and SD4 are all-wheel-drive. The 2.0-litre diesel and petrol variants arrived with the 2015 facelift. Cabriolet variants (2016–2018) share the same engine range.
